mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier financial conditions into equity valuation through a lower discount rate, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next month when the policy path is falling rather than flat.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are not positive more often than negative when FEDFUNDS is falling month-over-month, or if the signal's rolling 2-year hit rate falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above a recent threshold signals weakening household income growth and higher recession odds, which depresses risk appetite and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Reject if SPX delivers positive 20d returns in a majority of episodes after UNRATE crosses above 4.7%, or if the crossing event has no negative conditional edge versus the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized uptrend in RSAFS and GDPC1 indicates broad demand acceleration that supports corporate revenue expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over roughly one quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if joint positive changes in RSAFS and GDPC1 do not correspond to above-median 60d SPX returns, or if either series alone explains the effect without the interaction.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in INDPRO with stable or falling CPIAUCSL implies improving real output without renewed inflation pressure, which supports margins and reduces the need for tighter policy, creating positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with rising INDPRO and non-accelerating CPIAUCSL do not outperform the unconditional 20d SPX baseline, or if inflation rising with output growth produces the same sign.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST below its recent trend signals housing demand deterioration and tighter credit transmission, which typically leads equity markets lower over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 5% from its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if 20d SPX returns are not negative on average after HOUST falls more than 5% below its 3-month moving average, or if the threshold event is followed by positive returns more than 60% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth turns negative while UNRATE is rising, labor income momentum is deteriorating and consumption expectations weaken, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS/UNRATE signal does not predict below-median 60d SPX returns, or if the effect vanishes when controlling for either labor series alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, a high or rising FEDFUNDS rate compresses equity valuation multiples and tightens liquidity, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:policy rate was near zero in the snapshot but the mechanism is activated only after tightening begins
failure_condition: Reject if, once FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, the average 20d SPX return is not materially below the unconditional mean, or if positive returns dominate in tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS rises while UNRATE is falling, improving retail demand coincides with stronger labor income expectations, supporting earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Reject if joint RSAFS increases and UNRATE declines do not forecast positive 5d SPX returns, or if the signal is no better than the separate RSAFS and UNRATE effects.
